content.content: src/utils.ts:7:  for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++) {

content.text:
Perfect! I've found the bug. Here's my report:

## Bug Found: Off-by-One Error in Array Iteration

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775461930616-7maloq/src/utils.ts`

**Line:** 7

**Buggy Code:**
```typescript
for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++) {
```

**Problem:**
The loop condition uses `i < items.length - 1` instead of `i < items.length`. This causes the loop to stop one iteration before the last element, meaning the last item in the array is never processed.

**Example:**
- For an array of 3 items `["hello", "world", "foo"]`:
  - The loop runs with `i = 0, 1` (stops before `i = 2`)
  - Item at index 2 (`"foo"`) is never processed
  - Expected output: `["HELLO", "WORLD", "FOO"]`
  - Actual output: `["HELLO", "WORLD"]`

**Tests Confirming the Bug:**
The test file at `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775461930616-7maloq/tests/utils.test.ts` has three tests for `processItems` that would catch this bug:
- Line 5-11: Tests that all items are processed (`["hello", "world", "foo"]` → `["HELLO", "WORLD", "FOO"]`)
- Line 13-15: Tests a single item (would fail because loop doesn't run at all when `items.length = 1`)
- Line 17-19: Tests two items (would pass because the first item is processed)

**Fix:**
Change line 7 from:
```typescript
for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++) {
```
to:
```typescript
for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) {
```

The `sum` function in the same file uses a for-of loop which correctly handles all array items, so it doesn't have this issue.

```typescript
for (let i = 0; i < items.length - 1; i++) {
```

  - Actual output: `["HELLO", "WORLD"]`
